_description: Manage n8n workflows: list, activate, deactivate, update, deploy

_update_workflow._description: Fetch, modify, push back. Settings field is REQUIRED.
_gotchas: Extra settings fields (availableInMCP, binaryMode) cause validation errors
Only send: name, nodes, connections, settings in the PUT body
Strip everything else from the GET response before PUTting back
_pattern: 2. Edit the JSON (modify node params, add nodes, etc.)

_reactivate_workflow._description: Deactivate then reactivate a workflow. Use when a workflow is stuck, a node was updated, or you need to reset webhook listeners. Without this pattern, n8n may not pick up changes.
_gotchas: Must deactivate BEFORE reactivating. Activating an already-active workflow is a no-op.
Add a 1-2 second pause between deactivate and activate to avoid race conditions with external triggers (Zapier, webhooks).
If the workflow has a Catch Hook (like Zapier step 1), the external service may fire during the gap. Keep the pause short.
After reactivation, verify active status in the response: {"active": true}
_when_to_use: Workflow updated but changes not taking effect
Webhook listener seems dead (external service gets timeouts)
Node configuration changed and you need n8n to re-register
Race condition with external triggers (deactivate, wait, reactivate)
